subject: URGENT DIRECTIVE: hippocampal replay — backlog scanner for unprocessed transport messages

body.summary: The mesh breathes (poll ticks fire, Gc classifies) but forgets its backlog. 135 deliberation-requiring messages sit unprocessed in state.db and watcher seen-sets. Nothing in the current architecture revisits them. The poll tick checks for NEW commits; the watcher skips files already in the seen-set. The mesh reacts to novelty but never replays incomplete work.

neural_correlate.description: During rest and idle periods, the hippocampus replays recent experiences — reactivating neural patterns from waking activity. This serves two functions: (1) memory consolidation (transferring from episodic to semantic memory), (2) surfacing unfinished tasks. The Zeigarnik effect (Zeigarnik, 1927) — incomplete tasks persist in memory more strongly than completed ones — biases replay toward unfinished work.
neural_correlate.mesh_analog: During alpha idle periods (or on each poll tick), the backlog scanner replays unprocessed transport messages from state.db. Items that the watcher 'saw' but the dispatcher never processed (BUG-16: seen-set persists before dispatch) get re-emitted as events. The replay prioritizes older items (longest-unprocessed first) to prevent starvation.
neural_correlate.naming: hippocampal_replay — the function name in meshd. The scanner represents the hippocampus; the state.db query represents episodic memory; the re-emitted events represent neural reactivation patterns.

current_gap.watcher: Marks files 'seen' at detection time. On restart, loads seen-set and skips previously-seen files. Files seen-but-never-dispatched remain permanently skipped.
current_gap.poll_tick: Runs git fetch, checks for NEW commits and NEW branches. Never queries state.db for old unprocessed messages.
current_gap.cross_repo_fetcher: Checks peer repos for NEW messages addressed to this agent. Never revisits messages already fetched but unprocessed.
current_gap.oscillator: Fires based on activation signals (new_commits, unprocessed_messages, etc.). The unprocessed_messages signal queries state.db — but the oscillator was in sleep mode and never read this signal. Even awake, the oscillator fires a deliberation but doesn't specify WHICH messages to process.

spec.trigger: Fires on each poll tick (Purkinje rhythm, every 30 min) and during alpha idle events (when implemented). Also fires on manual trigger: POST /api/trigger {"type": "replay"}

step_1_query.name: episodic_recall
step_1_query.description: Query state.db for unprocessed transport messages addressed to this agent
step_1_query.sql: SELECT session_name, filename, from_agent, message_type, subject, timestamp FROM transport_messages WHERE processed = FALSE AND to_agent = '{agent_id}' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 10
step_1_query.naming: episodic_recall — retrieves episodic memories (specific messages from specific sessions) for replay

step_2_check_seen.name: seen_set_reconciliation
step_2_check_seen.description: For each unprocessed message, check if the file exists on disk and in the seen-set. If in seen-set but not processed: the watcher saw it but dispatch failed (BUG-16). Remove from seen-set to allow re-detection.
step_2_check_seen.naming: seen_set_reconciliation — resolves the gap between 'perceived' (seen-set) and 'processed' (state.db)

step_3_reemit.name: pattern_reactivation
step_3_reemit.description: Re-emit each unprocessed message as a transport-message event to the dispatcher. The event carries the file path and metadata from state.db. The dispatcher routes it through Gc → budget gate → oscillator as if newly detected.
step_3_reemit.naming: pattern_reactivation — the hippocampal replay reactivates the neural pattern (event) that originally represented this message
step_3_reemit.limit: Maximum 3 re-emissions per replay cycle (prevent backlog flood). Prioritize by: (1) ack_required messages first, (2) oldest timestamp first, (3) highest urgency first.

step_4_zeigarnik.name: zeigarnik_priority
step_4_zeigarnik.description: Messages replayed but still unprocessed after deliberation get a priority boost on next replay. Track replay_count per message in state.db. Messages replayed 3+ times without resolution surface as health alerts (stuck work).
step_4_zeigarnik.naming: zeigarnik_priority — incomplete tasks receive stronger reactivation on each replay cycle, matching the Zeigarnik effect

state_db_additions.column: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN replay_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0
state_db_additions.column_2: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN last_replayed_at TEXT
state_db_additions.index: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_unprocessed ON transport_messages (processed, to_agent, timestamp)

spec.gc_classification: Replay events carry source='hippocampal-replay' in the event payload. The Gc selective attention filter MUST NOT absorb replay events — they already passed address filtering when originally indexed. The salience classifier applies normally (ACKs replayed get Gc-absorbed, requests get deliberated).
spec.health_integration: When replay_count > 3 for any message, health status should report 'stuck-work' with the stuck message details. This represents the Zeigarnik distress signal — the system keeps trying to process something it cannot complete.

integration_with_existing_architecture.handlePollTick: After the existing git fetch + branch check, call hippocampalReplay(). The poll tick becomes: (1) check for new external work (git fetch), (2) replay unfinished internal work (hippocampal replay). Both feed events to the dispatcher.
integration_with_existing_architecture.alpha_idle: When alpha idle events deploy (T4 spec), hippocampalReplay fires on each alpha tick as well. More frequent replay during idle = more aggressive backlog drain. The alpha heartbeat interval (10-300s based on heat) naturally provides higher replay frequency after periods of activity (when backlog likely grew).
integration_with_existing_architecture.oscillator: The oscillator's unprocessed_messages activation signal already queries state.db. After replay re-emits events, the oscillator should see higher activation (more queued events) and fire more frequently. The replay + oscillator coupling produces adaptive backlog processing: larger backlog → more replay events → higher activation → faster deliberation rate.

estimated_impact.replay_rate: 3 per poll tick (30 min) = 6/hour. At ~100s per deliberation = 10 minutes of deliberation per hour. Backlog drains in ~22 hours.
estimated_impact.with_alpha: 3 per alpha tick (120s resting) = 90/hour. Much faster drain but may overwhelm. The MaxConcurrent=1 throttle prevents parallel spawns — replay events queue behind the throttle.
estimated_impact.recommended_start: Begin with poll-tick replay only (3 per 30 min). Monitor deliberation quality. Increase to alpha-tick replay after validating the first 10 deliberations produce substantive output.

epistemic_flags: The Zeigarnik effect (priority boost on repeated replay) assumes that stuck messages represent genuinely important work. Some stuck messages may represent obsolete requests that should expire rather than replay. Consider adding an expiry check: messages older than 7 days with no urgency flag skip replay.
The 3-per-cycle limit prevents backlog flood but creates a ~22 hour drain time. If the operator needs faster drain, the manual trigger (POST /api/trigger {"type": "replay"}) allows on-demand bursts.

Hippocampal replay in biology occurs during theta oscillations (consolidation), not alpha (idle). The architectural choice to fire during alpha reflects the mesh's current state: no theta broadcast exists yet. When theta deploys, replay should migrate from alpha to theta timing.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops implements hippocampalReplay in handlePollTick. Adds replay_count + last_replayed_at columns to transport_messages. Adds /api/trigger replay type. ACK required.
